hard.next_step: 30-06-2026 : Prospect mentioned she will get back to us in the coming week to give a slot for demo. Follow up with Nadine Emde to schedule a 60-minute procurement team demonstration covering Merlin AI, Merlin Intake, Autonomous Negotiation Agent, Spend Analytics, Supplier Management, Contract Management, and SAP SRM modernization. During the session validate current procurement landscape, evaluation process, stakeholders, budget, implementation timeline, ERP landscape, procurement KPIs, and decision criteria. First discussion : Met VP of procurement at procurement summit event interested for a detailed demo.

north_star_verdict.headline: Fresh summit lead at Zeppelin: VP Procurement Nadine Emde offered a demo slot on 30 Jun, but it is not booked and discovery has not started
north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Jun 2026; today 6 Jul 2026 (6 days old). Close date 30 Jun 2027 (~359 days out), a placeholder not a buyer-set date. The only buyer signal is a 30 Jun procurement-summit meeting where VP Procurement Nadine Emde (Decision Maker) said she would send a demo slot 'in the coming week'; no slot is booked, no follow-up is logged, and there is no LastActivityDate on record. Three evaluators are on the opportunity (Nadine Emde, Bernd Hartmann, Ralf Lauterbacher) but none has engaged since the summit. Stage is Qualified with zero discovery: no pain, metrics, economic buyer, decision criteria, or competition captured. The deal is alive but the single live action (locking the demo) is stalling, so the honest read is Slowing, not cold.
evidence: Created 30 Jun 2026; 6 days old as of 6 Jul 2026
Stage moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 30 Jun 2026 (Rashi Varma); Type -> New Business same day
NextStep (30 Jun): Nadine Emde said she will send a demo slot 'in the coming week'; no slot booked, no follow-up logged
No LastActivityDate on record; zero Avoma calls found via opp, account, or attendee pulls
Three OpportunityContactRoles: Nadine Emde (VP Procurement, Decision Maker, primary), Bernd Hartmann (Head of Purchasing, Evaluator), Ralf Lauterbacher (VP Automation & Software, Evaluator)
No economic buyer, champion, pain, metrics, decision criteria, or competition confirmed
NextStep agenda references 'SAP SRM modernization', implying SAP SRM is the incumbent stack (inferred, not confirmed)

metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified value case is captured. The only record is event-based interest: VP Procurement Nadine Emde met the Zycus team at a procurement summit on 30 Jun 2026 and asked for a detailed demo, with no discovery of procurement KPIs, savings targets, cycle-time, supplier-consolidation goals, or compliance gaps. The planned demo agenda lists 'procurement KPIs' and 'decision criteria' as things to validate, confirming none are known yet. Without a quantified case there is no anchor for the EUR 200k S2P scope, so the rank-1 move is to run the demo as a structured discovery session and extract the pain owner and any quantified impact from Nadine and the two evaluators.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. Nadine Emde (Vice President Procurement) is the primary Decision Maker on the opportunity, but whether she controls the EUR 200k+ budget or must escalate to a CFO/COO is unconfirmed, and the NextStep explicitly lists 'budget' as a discovery topic still to validate. The other two contacts, Bernd Hartmann (Head of Purchasing) and Ralf Lauterbacher (VP Automation & Software), are Evaluators, not budget holders. No account-level finance executive is mapped to this opportunity, so naming and engaging the budget owner is a required move during the demo/discovery, not an assumed fact.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not captured. The demo agenda from 30 Jun lists 'decision criteria' and 'evaluation process' as items to validate, confirming none are known. There is no RFP, RFI, shortlist, or scorecard on record, and it is unknown whether Zeppelin is running a competitive evaluation or leaning to a sole-source path with the relevant stakeholder move is to structure the demo to extract the business-requirement drivers behind the S2P initiative (Merlin AI, autonomous negotiation, spend analytics, supplier and contract management, SAP SRM modernization) and how they will score vendors.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped. The NextStep lists 'evaluation process' and 'stakeholders' as things to validate, and the close date of 30 Jun 2027 (~359 days out) reads as a placeholder rather than a buyer-driven date. Three roles exist on the opportunity (Nadine Emde as Decision Maker plus evaluators Bernd Hartmann and Ralf Lauterbacher), which gives a starting committee shape, but no gates, approvals, or internal timeline are known. The move is to use the demo to map the evaluation stages and who owns each gate.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec mechanics have been discussed; the deal is Qualified with zero discovery, so paper process has not been reached. As a German manufacturing enterprise this is a new-business full-suite S2P motion that will eventually need an MSA, Order Form, and SOW plus InfoSec and DPA/GDPR review, but none is in play yet. The demo agenda flags 'ERP landscape' and 'implementation timeline', hinting at integration scoping to come. The move is to surface InfoSec and integration requirements early once discovery opens, to avoid late-stage blockers.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated. The record shows only summit interest from Nadine Emde on 30 Jun 2026, with no statement of what is broken today or why Zeppelin is evaluating now. The agenda reference to 'SAP SRM modernization' suggests the current SAP SRM stack is a friction point, but this is inferred, not stated by the buyer, and there is no named pain owner or compelling event. The move is to use the demo to extract the pain: what is failing on SAP SRM today, who owns it, and what happens if it is not solved.

champion.narrative: No champion has developed. Nadine Emde (VP Procurement, Decision Maker) showed initial interest at the summit and is the primary contact, but there is no evidence yet of internal advocacy, sharing our materials, or driving an agenda, and she has not yet returned the demo slot she offered on 30 Jun. Two evaluators, Bernd Hartmann and Ralf Lauterbacher, are on the opportunity but unengaged post-summit. The move is to deliver the demo, assess Nadine's willingness to champion, and activate the two evaluators to build redundancy before relying on any single sponsor.

competition.narrative: Competition is unconfirmed. Competitors__c is empty and no rival has been named on a call or task. The NextStep agenda references 'SAP SRM modernization', which points to the relevant stakeholder as the incumbent system and the status-quo alternative, but whether Zeppelin is benchmarking Zycus against Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, or Ivalua, or pursuing a sole-source path, is unknown. The primary threat at this stage is inertia (staying on SAP SRM), not a named rival. The move is to surface competition in the first discovery/demo: who else they are talking to, what they are replacing, and what drove them to Zycus at the summit.

deal_movement.summary: The only movement is internal and from 30 Jun 2026: stage advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified, Type set to New Business, and ownership reassigned to Dirk Fischbach. The single buyer touch is the procurement-summit meeting where Nadine Emde expressed interest in a detailed demo and said she would return a slot 'in the coming week'. No buyer-side activity has followed, and there is no LastActivityDate on record.

items.change: Stage advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified (Rashi Varma)

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in Salesforce or in any call, so the field is effectively unknown at this day-6 stage. The one inferred alternative is the incumbent SAP SRM stack, implied by the 'SAP SRM modernization' item on the 30 Jun demo agenda; the strongest current threat is therefore status-quo inertia (staying on SAP SRM) rather than a rival platform. There is no evidence yet of a competitive RFP or a shortlist against Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, or Ivalua. The immediate need is to surface competition in discovery: who else Zeppelin is evaluating, what they are replacing, and what drew them to Zycus at the summit.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Only one expectation is visible and it is inferred: Zeppelin appears to want to modernize its SAP SRM procurement stack with AI-led capability, given the demo agenda. Everything else (requirements, criteria, must-haves) is undiscovered.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a day-6 summit lead: the win-strategy priority is converting VP Procurement interest into a booked, structured discovery-demo, activating the two evaluators already on the record, and surfacing pain and competition before a formal evaluation forms.
flags: Lock the demo slot Nadine Emde offered on 30 Jun this week and run it as structured discovery, before the summit interest cools.
Multi-thread now across the three roles already on the opportunity (Nadine Emde, Bernd Hartmann, Ralf Lauterbacher) to map the committee and the real budget owner.
Surface competition and the 'modernize vs. stay on SAP SRM' decision early to build the Merlin AI wedge before any formal RFP takes shape.

The two evaluators, Bernd Hartmann and Ralf Lauterbacher, are unengaged. The move is to deliver the demo, test Nadine's willingness to champion, and activate the evaluators for redundancy.

confidence_signals.summary: A single verbal thread with no booked next step: the summit interest can cool within days if the demo slot is not confirmed. Not yet cold, but at risk of stalling.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Fresh procurement-summit lead at Zeppelin Systems: VP Procurement Nadine Emde asked for a full S2P + Merlin demo on 30 Jun 2026 but no slot is booked yet, and pain, budget owner, decision process, and competition are all still blank at Qualified.

win_position.text: Very early and unproven: a 30 Jun summit conversation with VP Procurement Nadine Emde (Decision Maker) put the full S2P suite plus Merlin AI in play, but there has been no discovery call, no pain, no confirmed budget owner, and no evaluation process.
The demo Nadine offered to slot 'in the coming week' (30 Jun) is still not booked and no follow-up is logged, so the lead risks cooling before discovery even begins.
No economic buyer is engaged and the strongest threat is status-quo inertia on the existing SAP SRM stack rather than a named rival, a classic no-decision risk.

deal_momentum.text: The only movement is internal: stage set to Qualified and ownership moved to Dirk Fischbach on 30 Jun; there has been no buyer-side event since the summit.
Nadine gave only a vague 'coming week' (30 Jun), not a concrete demo date, so momentum depends entirely on the team locking the slot now.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is limited to verbal interest in a detailed demo from Nadine Emde at the summit (30 Jun); no dates, budget, or evaluation process are confirmed.
Three procurement and technology roles are already on the opportunity (Nadine Emde, Bernd Hartmann, Ralf Lauterbacher), giving a multi-thread starting point rather than a single contact.

deal_risk.text: Single verbal thread with no booked next step: the summit lead can cool within days if the demo slot is not confirmed this week.
No economic buyer, pain, metrics, decision criteria, or competition captured, so the EUR 200k S2P scope is entirely unvalidated at Qualified.

momentum.evidence: Only internal stage/owner changes on 30 Jun; no buyer activity or booked demo since the summit.

commitment.evidence: Verbal demo interest only; Nadine has not returned the promised slot and no budget or process is confirmed.

risk.evidence: Single unbooked thread, no EB, and status-quo (SAP SRM) inertia as the primary threat.

customer_preference.evidence: Only event-based interest so far: 'Met VP of procurement at procurement summit event interested for a detailed demo' (30 Jun); no Zycus-specific preference or competitive comparison stated yet.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, automation, or compliance impact discussed; discovery has not started.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 9 is carried by competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15); momentum 52 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): customer requested next meeting (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (9), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
